name: Mews
description: Mews is the operating system for modern hospitality, providing a cloud-native platform that connects property management (PMS), point-of-sale (POS), revenue management (RMS via Atomize), embedded payments, and 1000+ integrations. It powers 15,000+ properties across 85 countries with 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers.
prague_connection: Founded in Prague by Richard Valtr while building a hotel there. Core engineering and product teams remain in Prague. The Emblem Prague Hotel was the first Mews customer.
ai_details: Uses AI for dynamic pricing and demand forecasting through its Atomize RMS acquisition. Acquired the DataChat team to double down on AI. Revenue management system leverages machine learning to optimize hotel pricing in real time. Platform automates reservation management, check-in/check-out, housekeeping coordination, and payment processing.

products.name: Mews PMS
products.description: Cloud-native property management system for hotels, hostels, and extended stays. Handles reservations, guest check-in/out, housekeeping, accounting, and billing.

products.name: Mews POS
products.description: Point-of-sale system purpose-built for hotel restaurants and F&B operations, fully integrated with the PMS.

products.name: Atomize RMS
products.description: AI-powered revenue management system providing dynamic pricing, demand forecasting, and portfolio/group pricing optimization.

products.name: Mews Payments
products.description: Embedded payment processing with tokenization, automated reconciliation, multicurrency support, and terminal management.

products.name: Mews Marketplace
products.description: Integration marketplace with 1000+ hospitality apps and third-party connections.

products.name: Mews Booking Engine
products.description: Direct booking engine for hotel websites with guest self-check-in capabilities.

target_market: Hotels, hotel groups and chains, hostels, motels, extended stays, and independent properties worldwide

rounds.amount_note: EUR 101M; led by Kinnevik with participation from existing investors

revenue.revenue_note: Crossed $100M ARR in 2023; over $200M revenue in 2024 (Skift/ZoomInfo); VP Rev Ops job listing references scaling from $200M to $500M+ ARR

key_people.name: Richard Valtr
key_people.note: Ex-hotelier who founded Mews in 2012 while building a hotel in Prague

key_people.name: Matthijs Welle
key_people.note: Former hotelier; joined shortly after founding

key_people.name: Josef Starychfojtu
key_people.note: Joined Mews as a backend developer and rose to lead product and tech teams

key_people.name: Naomi
key_people.note: Leads People Team; company repeatedly wins Best Place to Work awards

acquisitions.name: Planet Winner
acquisitions.note: First acquisition; part of early expansion

acquisitions.name: Atomize
acquisitions.note: AI-powered revenue management system; now Mews RMS

customers.notable_count_note: 15,000 properties across 85 countries; 132,000+ monthly active hoteliers; 42.3M checked-in reservations in 2025

awards: Best Property Management System 2024, 2025, 2026 (Hotel Tech Report)
Best Places to Work in Hotel Tech 2024, 2025, 2026
hiring_notes: VP Revenue Operations (NYC), multiple engineering and sales roles visible. Job listings reference scaling from $200M to $500M+ ARR, indicating aggressive growth hiring.
tech_stack_notes: Cloud-native architecture; Open API; 1000+ marketplace integrations; mobile-first with kiosk check-in (3.2M kiosk check-ins in 2025)
data_sources: Brave Search: funding results (Vestbee, TechFundingNews, TheRecursive, EU-Startups, mews.com/press)
Brave Search: revenue results (Getlatka, Skift, ZoomInfo, SaaStock, Notion Capital)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Mixed signals — some differentiation but unclear long-term moat against foundation model capabilities.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Limited public information on market traction.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No public funding information. Sustainability unclear.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Mixed risk profile — some protective factors but also significant vulnerabilities.
